com.mentor.chs.plugin.drc

Interface IXDRCheck

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXPlugin
    All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXFunctionBuildListDRCheck, IXFunctionDesignDRCheck, IXHarnessDRCheck, IXIntegratorDRCheck, IXLogicBuildListDRCheck, IXLogicDRCheck, IXPlatformDRCheck, IXSBOMDRCheck, IXTopologyDRCheck

    @NotToBeImplementedDirectly
public interface IXDRCheck
extends IXPlugin
    Parent class for all DRCs. You should not implement this class directly. You should implement one of its children, for example, IXIntegratorDRCheck or IXLogicDRCheck. This will allow Capital to associate your plug-in with the correct design type. If you have developed a DRC that is compatible with more than one design type, all you need to do is implement all the interfaces concerned.

    DRC can also be configured to run on different running modes(MANUAL, BACKGROUND, ONSAVE etc.,) and design abstraction by implementing IXDRCheckAdvancedConfiguration along with this class. If a DRC does not implement IXDRCheckAdvancedConfiguration it will be available for all running modes except ONSAVE and BACKGROUND modes, design abstraction based on getDefaultAvailability() method, and the DRC will have the severity given by the getSeverity() method, regardless of running mode and design abstraction

    NOT TO BE USED DIRECTLY.
    See Also:
    IXDRCheckAdvancedConfiguration

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Interface and Description
      static class  IXDRCheck.Severity
      The severity of the DRC

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void begin(IXDRCViolationReporter reporter)
      Called at the start of the DRC check operation on a particular design for all running modes except for the BACKGROUND mode in which case it is called for each object.
      void check(IXDRCViolationReporter reporter, IXObject object)
      Execute the DRC check.
      void end(IXDRCViolationReporter reporter)
      Called at the end of the DRC check operation on a particular design for all running modes except for the BACKGROUND mode in which case it is called for each object.
      boolean getDefaultAvailability()
      Get the default availability of this check.
      IXDRCheck.Severity getDefaultSeverity()
      Get the default severity of this check.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXPlugin

        getDescription, getName, getVersion

    • Method Detail

      • begin

        void begin(IXDRCViolationReporter reporter)
        Called at the start of the DRC check operation on a particular design for all running modes except for the BACKGROUND mode in which case it is called for each object.
        Parameters:
        reporter - - this is the object that should be used to report any violations to.

      • check

        void check(IXDRCViolationReporter reporter,
           IXObject object)
        Execute the DRC check. Each design will contain a number of objects, like IXConnectors and IXWires. This method will be called several times on each of the objects within the design.
        Parameters:
        reporter - - this is the object that should be used to report any violations to.
        object - - this is the object within the design to check. For example this may be an IXDevice or an IXConnector. Use 'instanceof' if you are only interested in a particular type of object.

      • end

        void end(IXDRCViolationReporter reporter)
        Called at the end of the DRC check operation on a particular design for all running modes except for the BACKGROUND mode in which case it is called for each object.
        Parameters:
        reporter - - this is the object that should be used to report any violations to.

      • getDefaultSeverity

        IXDRCheck.Severity getDefaultSeverity()
        Get the default severity of this check.
        Returns:
        the default severity of this check.

      • getDefaultAvailability

        boolean getDefaultAvailability()
        Get the default availability of this check. Is the check enabled in the DRC GUI by default.
        Returns:
        the default availability of this check.
